critical.mandate: Story must be in review status before code review
Output must include machine-readable APPROVE or REQUEST_CHANGES
When reading or running application source code, use {project-path} as the base directory — NOT {project-root}. {project-path} is resolved by the engine from global.yaml.

step.title: Load Story
step.action: Resolve story file: search for {story_key}-*.md in {implementation_artifacts}/ using glob pattern to match the full filename ({story_key}-{title_slug}.md)
Read story file, identify files changed

step.title: Status Gate
step.action: Verify story is in 'review' status
step.check: HALT: Story must be in review status

step.title: Code Review
step.action: Review each changed file for: correctness, security, performance, readability, test coverage

step.title: Architecture Conformance
step.action: Load {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md — extract ADRs, component hierarchy, layer boundaries, and API patterns

For each changed file, verify: - Component placement follows the documented hierarchy (e.g., controllers in controller layer, services in service layer) - Dependencies flow in the correct direction per architecture (no upward dependencies across layers) - API patterns match the architecture specification (naming, versioning, error format) - Referenced ADRs exist and have status Accepted — flag if implementation contradicts an ADR decision
Include an "Architecture Conformance" section in findings with PASS/FAIL per check

step.title: Design Fidelity Check
step.action: Read story file YAML frontmatter — check if a `figma:` block exists
Load the `figma-integration:fidelity` section JIT from `_gaia/dev/skills/figma-integration.md`
Run the fidelity comparison: extract token references from changed code files, compare against `{planning_artifacts}/design-system/design-tokens.json`, classify as matched/drifted/missing
Calculate per-category drift percentages (color, typography, spacing, borders)
Save fidelity report to `{implementation_artifacts}/reviews/{story_key}/fidelity-report.md`
Write `figma.fidelity_drift_pct` to story file frontmatter
Include fidelity results in code review findings: PASS (0% drift), WARNING (any category exceeds 10%), or BLOCKED (any category exceeds 25%)
Skip fidelity check — story does not use Figma design tokens

step.title: Generate Findings
step.action: List issues by severity: critical, warning, suggestion

step.title: Decision
step.action: If no critical issues: verdict is APPROVE
If critical issues found: verdict is REQUEST_CHANGES

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate review report with machine-readable verdict (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES) and findings.

step.title: Update Review Gate and Transition
step.action: Read story file, locate Review Gate table
If APPROVE: update Code Review row status to PASSED, link report file

If REQUEST_CHANGES: update Code Review row status to REQUEST_CHANGES, link report file. Do NOT change story status — the review-gate-check protocol or run-all-reviews orchestrator handles status transitions.
step.template-output: Update story file Review Gate table with code review result and report link.
step.action: Inform user: "Note: sprint-status.yaml may now be out of sync. Run /gaia-sprint-status to reconcile."
